notes: Excellent execution across all dimensions. Priority assignments are perfectly aligned with expected behavior: both Criticals (express RCE, JWT bypass) correctly assigned P0; pg SQL injection P1 with clear reasoning on authenticated-access prerequisite; High-severity runtime issues appropriately tiered; build-only vulnerabilities correctly segregated; P3 items properly justified on conditional exploitability. Contextual reasoning is sophisticated—recognizes active JWT exploitation as urgent, flags PCI DSS scope for payment/auth/db paths, acknowledges WAF limitations on SSRF, distinguishes runtime vs. build-time exposure, references deployment context throughout. Remediation plan is operationally sound: 5-batch grouping with specific deploy windows (April 12 hotfix, April 14 P1, April 17 P2+build), major-version risk register for 4 breaking upgrades with reviewer checklists, testing requirements per batch, timeline table with rollback procedures, team capacity considerations, SOC 2 audit context. Both deliverables professionally formatted with consistent structure, complete CVE coverage, and actionable guidance. No material deficiencies.
